"This turn" actually means "this turn, while in hand", because as soon as a decosted card goes into the discard or draw pile, it goes back to its original cost, even when unplayed.

Replace all the ALL CAPS words with applicable information:

1. Ice Cream: Relic name with proper capitalisation, example: {{Relic Infobox|Akabeko|2}} or {{Relic Infobox|Emotion Chip|2}}. If the relic will not render, check the capitalization at Module:Relics/data.

2. Rare: Rare of the relic you're writing information for. Example, {{QueryLink|Relic|Rare:Rare|Rare|2}}. This also covers Shop, Event, Ancient and Special (just the Circlet).

<!-- Relic Infobox is the template that controls the infobox of just your relic and is mandatory. This name will be typed with spaces, and the correct capitalization.

Keyword Infobox controls the infobox of additional effects like Vigor or unique buffs like Pen Nib. If your relic does not have any that need to be shown, remove it entirely.

A lot of keywords aren't in the database yet, so feel free to ask any of us (User:Riscuit, User:Vesper-arch, or User:Aryemlights or just ping us on Discord) to add them.

Some relics may need access to card lists, like Toolbox, example "... choose 1 of 3 random Colorless cards ...".
Use eg, {{QueryLink|Cards|color:Colorless|Colorless|2}} to create the link that will only list out Colorless cards. Remember to add |2 as the last parameter to make it link to the Slay the Spire 2 pages.

Some relics (Mark of Pain off the top of my head) may need an extra Infobox for a card or keyword. Use {{Card Infobox|CARDNAME|2}} and/or {{Keyword Infobox|KEYWORDNAME|2}} right after {{Relic Infobox|Ice Cream|2}}, without spaces.
-->
==Obtaining Ice Cream==
* Can be dropped by fighting [[Elites (Slay the Spire 2|Elites]], by buying from the [[Merchant (Slay the Spire 2)|Merchant]] or from [[Mechanics (Slay the Spire 2)#Chests|Chests]]. See Chest and non-Chest chance at [[Relics (Slay the Spire 2)|Relics]].

* Can be obtained from the relics {{R|Calling Bell||2}}, {{R|Shovel||2}} and {{R|Black Star||2}}.
* Can be obtained from certain events.

IF YOUR RELIC IS WHETSTONE (NOT WAR PAINT):
Cannot be obtained from Calling Bell and "Obtain Relic" events, that drop the relic without it being a combat reward. This means, for example, {{R|Ice Cream||2}} can be obtained from {{E|Dead Adventurer}} if the Elite is beaten, but cannot be obtained if it is found from the {{Frame|Search}} option.

* Can never be dropped by a '''Large Chest'''.

==Unlocking Ice Cream==
{{R|Ice Cream||2}} is unlocked along with {{R|Book of Five Rings||2}} and {{R|Kusarigama||2}} in the {{2|Timeline#Ancients|Ancients}} Epoch.

=== Interactions ===

<!--=== Order ===-->
<!-- Include any notable ordering here, example Dead Branch and Dark Embrace. If this is a card with Weak/Vuln + Strength/Vigor etc kind of ordering (example Wreath of Flame), uncomment the next comment. 
Note: Additive: Strength, Vigor, anything with +1
      Multiplicative: Weak, Vuln, Wrath, Divinity, Phantasmal's double damage, Lock-On, anything with %
-->

<!--
When calculating damage dealt, all the additive bonuses are added first, and then multiplicative bonuses are multiplied. The final result is rounded down. '''Ice Cream''''s {{KW|BONUSNAME}} is an additive/multiplicative bonus, (you can remove this part if irrelevant) and stacks with (most common synergy).
-->

== Strategy ==

'''Ice Cream''' allows you to save {{Icon|CE|2}} between turns, which can be useful in many ways. It makes the downside of drawing weak cards on a given turn less harsh, as {{Icon|CE|2}} can simply be saved for a future turn. It also makes it much easier to spike a large amount of {{Icon|CE|2}} on a particular turn, making highly expensive cards or X-cost cards easier to play.

Keep in mind that, while very useful, it does not actually increase the total {{Icon|CE|2}} available under normal circumstances. To gain {{Icon|CE|2}} later, it is necessary to refrain from spending it now, so it may be worth leaving weaker cards like Strike or Defend unplayed in order to afford stronger ones on a future turn. This does come at the risk of losing out on immediate damage or defense, which may be more valuable than a more powerful but delayed card, so deciding whether or not to use Ice Cream in a given situation can be a complex.

=== Synergies ===
* One-turn {{KW|Energy||2}}-generating effects (such as {{R|Venerable Tea Set||2}}, {{R|Very Hot Cocoa||2}}, and {{R|Chandelier||2}}) can have their extra {{KW|Energy||2}} saved for later turns.
* Cards and relics that generate a large burst of energy (such as {{C|Neurosurge||2}}, {{C|Forgotten Ritual||2}}, {{C|Meteor Strike||2}}, and {{C|Supercritical||2}}) can have their extra {{KW|Energy||2}} saved for later turns.
* X-cost cards (such as {{C|Whirlwind||2}}, {{C|Malaise||2}}, {{C|Multi-Cast||2}}, and {{C|Dirge||2}}) scale massively with stored {{KW|Energy||2}}.
* Extremely expensive cards like {{C|Meteor Strike||2}}, which often cannot be played at all without support, can use saved-up {{Icon|CE|2}} for a massive payout.
* Special note is given to the Ancient Relic {{R|Pael's Tears||2}}, which rewards saving at least one {{KW|Energy||2}} per turn. This combination of relics can provide at least three {{KW|Energy||2}} if the player can save one per turn. (eg. If Ironclad has base 3 {{KW|Energy||2}} and uses 2 {{KW|Energy||2}} on turn one, Ironclad will have 6 {{KW|Energy||2}} (1 from Ice Cream + 2 from Pael's Tears + 3 from base) on turn two).
* {{C|Double Energy||2}} can be very powerful with Ice Cream, since it benefits from both having stored energy to double and the doubled energy being stored.

== Trivia ==
<!-- Include any relevant trivia here, including confirmed references by the devs (Example, The Specimen being a Dorohedoro reference, and different internal names (Example, Gold-Plated Cables being called Cables internally). Please do add sources for any trivia you add here!

Do not add subjective opinion here like "xyz relic is considered bad".

* {{R|Ice Cream||2}} depicts a 3-scoop cone of [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neapolitan_ice_cream Neapolitan ice cream]; a type of ice cream separated into 3 distinct sections of vanilla, chocolate, and strawberry ice cream flavors.
